x-enumeration-literals.name: Account-Level Tracking
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Monitoring individual customer accounts to ensure accurate balances, transaction processing, and compliance with account rules.

Comment:
Key Items Tracked:
Account balances: Amounts available and posted in deposits or checking accounts.
Transaction status: Whether a payment or transfer is pending, completed, or failed.
Interest accrual: Interest earned or payable on the account.
Fees and charges: Service fees, overdraft fees, penalties.
Limit utilization: Credit or overdraft limits and how much is used.

Example: Monitoring a checking account to ensure the customers daily balance is accurate after deposits and withdrawals.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Loan & Credit Tracking
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Monitoring all aspects of lending, including borrower obligations, repayment performance, and collateral status.

Comment:
Key Items Tracked:
Outstanding principal: Remaining loan amount.
Accrued interest: Interest accumulated over time.
Payment history: Record of repayments and defaults.
Collateral value: Security pledged against the loan.
Credit exposure: Total amount lent to a single borrower or sector.

Example: Tracking a corporate loan portfolio to ensure borrowers are repaying on schedule and collateral remains sufficient.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Deposits & Funding Tracking
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Monitoring bank liabilities, funding sources, and deposit inflows/outflows.

Comment:
Key Items Tracked:
Deposit balances: Total deposits across products or accounts.
Withdrawal trends: Patterns of cash withdrawals over time.
Term deposits: Maturity dates and interest accruals.
Funding concentration: Exposure to large depositors or institutions.

Example: Monitoring customer deposits to maintain sufficient liquidity for daily withdrawals.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Investment & Asset Tracking
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Monitoring the banks assets, investments, and their market value.

Comment:
Key Items Tracked:
Securities portfolio: Bonds, equities, mutual funds.
Market value: Daily valuation of assets.
Portfolio performance: Returns and gains/losses.
Liquidity of assets: Ability to convert assets to cash.
Asset allocation: Distribution across different asset classes.

Example: Tracking the banks investment portfolio to ensure it meets performance and liquidity targets.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Risk & Exposure Tracking
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Monitoring exposure to potential financial losses due to credit, market, operational, or liquidity risks.

Comment:
Key Items Tracked:
Credit risk: Probability of borrower default.
Market risk: Sensitivity to interest rates, currencies, or equities.
Operational risk: Fraud, system errors, or process failures.
Liquidity risk: Ability to meet short-term obligations.
Concentration risk: Exposure to a single borrower, sector, or instrument.

Example: Monitoring exposure to large corporate borrowers to prevent over-concentration in one sector.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Capital & Solvency Tracking
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Monitoring the banks financial strength and ability to meet long-term obligations.

Comment:
Key Items Tracked:
Shareholder equity: Bank-owned capital and retained earnings.
Tier 1 / Tier 2 capital: Regulatory capital categories.
Debt-to-equity ratio: Measure of solvency.
Regulatory ratios: Capital adequacy, leverage, liquidity coverage.

Example: Ensuring Basel III capital adequacy requirements are met.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Operational Tracking
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Monitoring day-to-day banking operations to ensure efficiency and smooth service delivery.

Comment:
Key Items Tracked:
Transaction volumes: Number and value of payments processed.
Branch / ATM performance: Cash availability, transactions handled.
Settlement status: Cleared vs pending interbank settlements.
Service efficiency: Cost-to-income ratio, loan processing time.

Example: Monitoring the number of payments processed daily to detect delays or bottlenecks.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Customer & Product Tracking
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Monitoring customer behavior and banking product performance for strategic and operational decisions.

Comment:
Key Items Tracked:
Product adoption: Accounts, loans, or cards issued.
Customer profitability: Revenue generated per customer.
Portfolio risk: Loan defaults by product type.
Fee generation: Fees collected by product category.

Example: Measuring profitability of credit cards versus savings accounts.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Forecast Tracking
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Monitoring expected future financial status using historical data and predictive models.

Comment:
Key Items Tracked:
Cash flow forecasts: Expected inflows and outflows.
Loan default projections: Predicted non-performing loans.
Deposit trends: Seasonal fluctuations in deposits.
Capital requirements: Projected regulatory capital needs.

Example: Forecasting liquidity requirements for the next quarter based on seasonal deposit trends.
